skill.name: ngx-hybrid-sales
skill.description: Sales framework for qualifying HYBRID prospects, preparing calls, handling objections, and tracking pipeline outcomes.
skill.summary: Qualifies HYBRID sales leads and supports structured call preparation.
skill.author: 270aldo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and seven weak cryptography patterns. Review found no executable command usage, no cryptographic logic, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration behavior in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged as External Command
low_findings.description: The static external command finding points to a Markdown fenced block that documents a sales call structure. It is not shell, Ruby, or executable command syntax, and no command injection vector is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are a Markdown code fence containing numbered sales call stages. There is no interpreter directive, command name, variable expansion, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sales Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in descriptive sales content and objection handling text. No hash functions, cipher names, encryption routines, or credential handling are present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each referenced line is plain Spanish sales guidance or product copy. I found no code or semantic context indicating cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Sales teams need consistent qualification for HYBRID prospects and clear call preparation. This skill provides questions, scoring, objection responses, and pipeline metrics.
actual_capabilities: Qualifies leads between HYBRID and ASCEND using five discovery questions.
Scores prospects with weighted signals such as discipline, budget, urgency, and prior attempts.
Structures a 20 to 30 minute HYBRID sales call from rapport to close.
Provides responses for price, timing, product fit, and time objections.
Defines weekly pipeline metrics for qualified leads, calls, close rate, revenue, and churn.
Lists pre-call and post-call checklist items for sales follow-up.
limitations: It is specific to the HYBRID and ASCEND product positioning.
It does not integrate with CRM, payment, calendar, or email systems.
It does not verify medical, pricing, guarantee, or compliance claims.
It provides guidance only and cannot replace sales judgment.

use_cases.title: Qualify New Prospects
use_cases.description: Use the five-question framework to decide whether a lead fits HYBRID or ASCEND.

use_cases.title: Prepare Discovery Calls
use_cases.description: Build a call outline with discovery questions, offer framing, and likely objections.

use_cases.title: Review Pipeline Health
use_cases.description: Compare weekly activity against target metrics for leads, calls, close rate, and churn.

prompt_templates.title: Classify a Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HYBRID qualification framework to classify this prospect. Ask any missing questions before recommending HYBRID or ASCEND.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Sales Call
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30 minute HYBRID sales call plan for this lead. Include discovery, offer framing, objections, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Objections
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these objections from a HYBRID prospect. Draft direct responses that match the skill guidance and keep the sale ethical.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Pipeline Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this weekly HYBRID pipeline data against the target metrics. Identify weak stages and recommend concrete follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: A prospect has failed three fitness programs, rates discipline as four, prefers guidance, and can pay 200 dollars monthly.
output_examples.output: The lead is a strong HYBRID candidate. The key reasons are repeated failed attempts, low discipline, preference for guidance, and sufficient budget.

output_examples.input: A prospect says HYBRID is too expensive after hearing the three month price.
output_examples.output: A useful response compares the cost with previous failed attempts, restates the guarantee, and asks what outcome would justify the investment.

output_examples.input: A sales team closed two HYBRID deals from four calls and eight qualified leads this month.
output_examples.output: The close rate is strong, but lead volume is below the weekly target. Increase qualified lead generation before optimizing call conversion.

best_practices: Use the five questions before recommending a product path.
Document every call result and next step in the pipeline.
Adapt objection responses to the prospect's real concern.
anti_patterns: Do not recommend HYBRID only because the price is higher.
Do not pressure prospects who lack budget or urgency.
Do not promise outcomes beyond the documented guarantee.

faq.question: What product does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports sales for HYBRID, a premium offer described as AI agents plus weekly human coaching.

faq.question: Can it choose between HYBRID and ASCEND?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses qualification questions and scoring signals to recommend HYBRID or ASCEND.

faq.question: Does it include a call script?
faq.answer: It includes a structured call flow with discovery, agitation, solution, offer, and close stages.

faq.question: Does it connect to a CRM?
faq.answer: No. It provides checklists and metrics, but it does not automate CRM updates.

faq.question: Can it handle pricing objections?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for price, timing, product fit, and time objections.

faq.question: Is this a general sales framework?
faq.answer: No. It is tailored to the HYBRID and ASCEND offers described in the skill.
